item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The minimum radius of 16 meters doesn't come into play, and people seem to get confused and think 16 meters is the maximum range. Maybe do away with it?

When I read the article title I went, "oh OK, it will be a stopwatch that stops time everywhere, and it turned out to be something different and original.i didn't care that much that it is not a hugely potent item. And I think you picked a great ending to give it an interesting closure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very interesting and enjoyable read! Would love to read more testing logs sometime.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heh. Noyadint

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for using a microwave to contain the thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: True, it wouldn't be a subjective "significance event" until he is effected by it. Perhaps if the phone rang and he got chewed out?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"This event occurred at exactly 11PM local time, or Midnight in ████████ time where Dr. ██████'s wife was living at the time. She was furious that he missed their anniversary."

I thought this thing only worked in a 16m radius. Even though it has some relevance to a researcher in the area, I don't think Dr. ██████ found his anniversary a "significance event" if he missed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I instantly thought Bernard's Watch when I read this, and the picture almost confirmed it for me, so I nearly didn't read it.

I was glad I did, though. It's a neat little artifact and I love its happy ending.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When I first saw this on the main list, I assumed boring "watch that stops time." Not sure if that has been done as an SCP, but I'm glad this isn't that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i love time related scps and this is no disappointment :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This. Something didn't exactly click for me when I read the article, and this comment made me realize what it is - it just isn't very interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The end with the microwave part made me smile, the stopwatch got it's own little happy ending.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Awww, the watch made a new friend.

I like the idea of an object which predicts something, but you have no idea what.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seems to me that the best way of containing it would be to put it in a shed which is surrounded on all sides by at least 16m of bare ground.

On ██/██/████, a civilian skydiver identified as [REDACTED] suffered a malfunction with his parachute and plummeted to his death, landing exactly 3.2 meters from the storage shed, coinciding with the end of an anomalously activated countdown.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dang, I'd only read up to 700 as of yet, and while mine is different than that one, I'm still sad it's not quite as unique an idea as I thought.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That would be SCP-1032

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Isn't there a countdown clock with, like, 100 hands that are all counting down "until" different things? And at completely different rates?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1244 is currently under observation in storage room ██, which shares a wall with the D-Class cafeteria kitchen.

Seems to me that the best way of containing it would be to put it in a shed which is surrounded on all sides by at least 16m of bare ground.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry, I'm new to the site and it wouldn't let me upload any images. If you know how to get the image in proper format I'll change it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The image used for this is far too large for the bizzarely small compression you've made Wikidot subject it to, and - perhaps more importantly, it's also hosted off-site. While I believe there are some problems with file uploading at the moment, the sandbox is still good for that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is more of an anomalous item than an object that is a threat to human normalcy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … am I the only one who thinks that this isn't that good at all? Aside from minor tonal issues, it's just yet another artifact (double points for being a watch) that we can't tell whether it predicts stuff or causes them to happen. I've seen better executions of this idea deleted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well I can see potential, so I think the Foundation would do more testing. One very curious thing I would test is whether the watch would detect a specific nature of event like disasters on television with news reports, preference over which event like whether it would follow an alarm, a scheduled event like the change in shifts or the anniversary.

While I see potential, the article itself is not too bad or good, so until than, no + or -, but I am curious about how it would turn out with some alterations.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think that because this thing can't really be left alone in a locked box, and because it activates by itself, it may need to be Euclid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed reading this article. While not creepy, you definitely made it interesting enough to keep my attention. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gotta say, I actually really like the ending of it syncing with the microwave by itself. It just seems… quaint.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this one

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I kind of like it, but what's that about syncing with the microwave, and why need an alarm for testing? I might've missed something.

Also log formatting could be touched up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure about this one. The effect feels kind of arbit|ary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good point, I'll fix asap

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For some reason I kinda like that it matches up with the microwave at the end, it's like it's happy about being left alone and has found a new hobby or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Chronos, perhaps?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, but I feel that this test report:

Subject: Digital clock with 61 seconds left till alarm.
Date: 01/21/██

SCP-1244 spun up to 60 seconds. When it reached zero, researcher █████ collapsed to the floor, dead of a ruptured brain aneurysm. Subsequently discovered medical reports from 90 days ago had given him roughly three months to live.
Note: It may just be me, but it feels like this was a little vindictive. It is my recommendation that we consider testing done on this particular object, I'd rather not piss it off any more and risk it interacting with any other SCPs in the facility. - Dr. ██████

could stand to be reworked. I can see the purpose of it (showing that the object can count down to nasty things) but the escalation between it and the previous log seems a bit much (missed anniversary, straight to death). Also could use some rewording to improve the tone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"I'll see you soon -C".

Convenience Clock engraved by an entity known as C!?

Spoilers
Hide

This has no significance whatsoever.
